SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLTF'S COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16; MEMORANDUM OF P/A IN SUPPORT THEREOF; APPENDIX OF EXHIBITS IN SUPPORT OF DEFT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE, PROOF OF SERVICE, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT JOHNSON, SCOTT DICKSON, PETER LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP HEARING SET FOR DEC-30-2008 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 301
EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER FOR CONTINUANCE, DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF FAERBER, RAINER J

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS SCOTT JOHNSON, LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP AND PETER DIXON'S MOTION FOR ORDER: (1) DECLARING PLAINTIFF A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT, (2) REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO POST SECURITY, AND (3) FOR A PRE-FILING ORDER PROHIBITING PLAINTIFF FROM FILING NEW LITIGATION PER CCP SECTIONS 391-391.7 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT BECAUSE HE HAS REPEATEDLY FILED NON-MERITORIOUS SUITS TO RELITIGATE ISSUES RESOLVED BY AN EARLIER SUIT AND HE HAS NOT AND CANNOT SHOW A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING IN THIS LITIGATION. TO THE EXTENT THIS CAUSE CAN CONTINUE AFTER RESOLUTION OF THE UNOPPOSED SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE, PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO POST SECURITY IN THE AMOUNT OF $2500. PLAINTIFF MAY NOT FILE A NEW LAWSUIT UNREPRESENTED BY AN ATTORNEY WITHOUT ADVANCE PERMISSION OF THE PRESIDING JUDGE AS REQUIRED BY CCP 391.7(A). THE CLERK OF COURT IS DIRECTED TO COMPLETE NECESSARY PAPERWORK TO ADVISE THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF THIS DETERMINATION IN ACCORDANCE WITH SECTION 391.7(E). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: JACQUELINE CHAN, CSR# 10276

DEFENDANT SCOTT JOHNSON, PETER DICKSON, LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SM ITH LLP'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLTF'S COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16; MEMORANDUM OF P/A IN SUPPORT THEREOF; APPENDIX OF EXHIBITS IN SUPPORT OF DEFT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN THAT THE COMPLAINT ARISES FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN NO LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: JACQUELINE CHAN, CSR# 10276 (301/PJB)

Notice Of Motion For Order: (1) Declaring Pltf A Vexatious Litigant, (2) Requiring Pltf To Post Security, And (3) For A Prefiling Order Prohibiting Pltf From Filing New Litigation Per Ccp Sections 391-391.7
Off Calendar Dec-29-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Duane Musfelt, Esq. of Lewis, Brisbois, et al, 415-438-6606 for defendant and moving party Rey Hassan

RULING:
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT SCOTT JOHNSON, LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, PETER DIXON'S Notice Of Motion For Order: (1) Declaring Pltf A Vexatious Litigant, (2) Requiring Pltf To Post Security, And (3) For A Prefiling Order Prohibiting Pltf From Filing New Litigation Per Ccp Sections 391-391.7. GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT BECAUSE HE HAS REPEATEDLY FILED NON-MERITORIOUS SUITS TO RELITIGATE ISSUES RESOLVED BY AN EARLIER SUIT AND HE HAS NOT AND CANNOT SHOW A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING IN THIS LITIGATION. TO THE EXTENT THIS CAUSE CAN CONTINUE AFTER RESOLUTION OF THE UNOPPOSED SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE. PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO POST SECURITY IN THE AMOUNT OF $2500. PLAINTIFF MAY NOT FILE A NEW LAWSUIT UNREPRESENTED BY AN ATTORNEY WITHOUT ADVANCE PERMISSION OF THE PRESIDING JUDGE AS REQUIRED BY CCP 391.7(a). THE CLERK OF COURT IS DIRECTED TO COMPLETE NECESSARY PAPERWORK TO ADVISE THE JUDICIAL COUNSEL OF THIS DETERMINATION IN ACCORDANCE WITH SECTION 391.7(e). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Jacqueline Chan, CSR# 10276 (301/PJB/ER)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp Section 473
Off Calendar Dec-11-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Rainer Faerber's Motion to Vacate Sanctions issued on November 4, 2008 to Plaintiff in Propria Persona, in the amount of $200.00 is scheduled this day. There being no appearance nor an objection tentative ruling to grant motion is adopted. Motion is ordered granted. Sanctions are ordered vacated. Moving party to submit a proposed order within five days. Proceedings not reported. (212)
